title: Congress passed the biggest housing bill in a generation. Trump is holding it hostage.
gist: Halina Bennet reports Trump canceled his signing of Congress's newly passed 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, holding the housing bill hostage until lawmakers also pass his SAVE America Act. The paywall cuts the article off after one sentence of substantive text.

insights.claim: Congress passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, described by Slow Boring as the biggest housing bill passed in a generation, but President Trump canceled his planned signing of it.
insights.speaker: Halina Bennet
insights.so_what: A major housing-supply bill is now stalled at the signing stage, creating timeline uncertainty for anyone positioned around federal housing policy.
insights.quote: canceled plans to sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act

insights.claim: Trump is withholding his signature on the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act until Congress also passes his separate SAVE America Act, using the housing bill as leverage for his own legislative priority.
insights.speaker: Halina Bennet
insights.so_what: The housing bill's enactment timeline now depends on progress of the unrelated SAVE America Act, a linkage worth monitoring for anyone tracking housing-policy catalysts.
insights.quote: he will not sign it until lawmakers pass his SAVE America Act
insights.locator: The president said he will not sign it
